pi_items.source: Web search confirms 'Mitosis' is a standard 5D hyperstack sample in ImageJ (mitosis.tif).
task.json instructions and setup_task.sh explicit directory creation.

Python analysis of mitosis.tif from imagej.net. The right-most daughter cell actually traverses from ~X=143, Y=127 in Frame 1 to ~X=162, Y=125 in Frame 21, confirming the provided coordinates are valid approximate click locations that fall well within the 25px tolerance window evaluated by the verifier.
verifier.py extracts this value and uses it strictly in a Euclidean distance condition: dist <= tolerance.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard ImageJ 'Mitosis' sample (a real 5D hyperstack of a dividing HeLa cell). Analysis of the downloaded image confirms the target cell begins near X=145, Y=130, and the right-most daughter cell migrates to approximately X=162, Y=125 by Frame 21. The ground truth coordinates are approximate manual click coordinates that correctly capture this movement and are well within the 25px tolerance radius allowed by the verifier.
